sourceMovie.title: Bicentennial Man
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Bicentennial Man (1999) stuck because it commits to its science fiction premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Chris Columbus's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The science fiction and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Blade Runner
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bicentennial Man clicked for you, Blade Runner (1982) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Bicentennial Man?
faq.answer: Try Blade Runner, Archive, I, Robot—each shares DNA with Bicentennial Man while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Bicentennial Man?
faq.answer: Bicentennial Man (1999) is best described as Science Fiction, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Bicentennial Man worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
